mega.desc: Travel to China without a visa! Learn about China\'s 240-hour transit visa-free policy, unilateral visa-free countries, and mutual visa exemption countries.
mega.title: Visa-Free Entry to China

mutual.desc: Citizens of both countries holding ordinary passports can usually travel to each other\'s country for short term without prior visa application.
mutual.title: List of Countries with Mutual Visa-Free Agreements

sidebar.portDesc1: Eligible: Citizens of 54 countries (e.g., US, UK, Japan)
sidebar.portDesc2: Requirement: Only available at 60 designated ports like Beijing, Shanghai, Guangzhou.
sidebar.areaDesc1: Allowed: Travel or activities within 24 provinces/municipalities including Beijing, Shanghai, Sichuan.
sidebar.areaDesc2: Prohibited: Staying outside the prescribed area.
sidebar.ruleTitle: Compliance: Strict Adherence to Time & Registration
sidebar.ruleDesc1: Time: Max stay 240h (starting from 00:00 the day after entry)
sidebar.ruleDesc2: Overstay: Must apply for residence permit in advance (only for emergencies/force majeure).
sidebar.ruleDesc3: Registration: Register accommodation within 24h (hotel or police station).
sidebar.ruleDesc4: Restricted: Only for short-term tourism/business; prohibited from working/studying.
sidebar.disclaimer: Disclaimer: Information provided is for reference only. For specific policies, please refer to the official NIA website. Policies subject to change.
sidebar.materialDesc2: Confirmed connecting ticket (flying to a third country within 240h, cannot return to origin)
sidebar.materialDesc3: Process: Submit documents at border inspection on arrival; entry granted after approval.

section.desc: China has expanded the 144-hour and newly introduced 240-hour transit visa-free policies to multiple countries. Eligible foreign travelers and business people can enter through designated ports and stay in designated areas for up to 6–10 days without prior visa application.
section.title: About the 240-Hour Transit Visa-Free Policy

unilateral.desc: Citizens of the following countries holding ordinary passports can enter China without a visa for tourism, business, and visiting relatives.
unilateral.title: List of Countries with Unilateral Visa-Free Entry

menu.desc: Upload photos to get translations, ingredients, and prices for easy ordering

faq.q1: How does ReadMenuAI generate dish images?
faq.a1: Our AI system analyzes the text description from the menu, cross-references it with a vast culinary database, and uses generative AI to match or create high-fidelity dish images for you.
faq.q2: Can I use it for non-Chinese menus?
faq.a2: Currently, we focus primarily on Chinese-to-English translation, but our system supports character recognition for multiple major languages.
faq.a3: Yes, we integrate with a real-time exchange rate API to provide accurate conversion between CNY and major foreign currencies based on latest rates.
faq.q4: Can I save my menu recognition records?
faq.a4: Registered users can view their full recognition history, including translations and generated images, in their personal dashboard.
faq.q5: Why are some dishes not recognized correctly?
faq.a5: Artistic fonts or messy handwriting can reduce accuracy. For best results, ensure good lighting and keep the menu flat when capturing.
faq.q6: How long does it take to process a menu?
faq.a6: Typically, the entire process of scanning, translating, and image generation takes only 10-15 seconds.
faq.q7: Do I need an internet connection?
faq.a7: Yes, the AI processing and image generation run in the cloud, so a stable internet connection is required.
faq.q8: How is ReadMenuAI different from standard translators?
faq.a8: We go beyond text translation by providing a visual experience, allowing you to see what the dish looks like alongside currency conversions.

dish1.name: 红烧鸡块
dish1.desc: A classic Chinese home-style dish. Chicken pieces are braised until tender and flavorful, with a glossy red sauce that's savory and slightly sweet.
dish1.ingredients: Key ingredients: Chicken, Potato, Green Pepper

dish2.name: 家常豆腐
dish2.desc: A common home-style dish featuring pan-fried tofu cubes stir-fried with pork and vegetables. Crispy on the outside, tender inside, savory with a hint of spice.
dish2.ingredients: Key ingredients: Tofu, Pork, Wood Ear Mushroom, Greens

dish3.name: 辣子鸡
dish3.desc: A Sichuan classic. Crispy chicken pieces stir-fried with generous amounts of dried chilies and Sichuan peppercorns. Numbing, spicy, and incredibly aromatic.
dish3.ingredients: Key ingredients: Chicken, Scallion, Ginger, Chili
dish3.allergens: ⚠️ Contains large amounts of chili and Sichuan peppercorn — very spicy and numbing; may contain peanuts

name.desc: AI-powered Chinese name generator for career, travel, or living in China
name.title: AI-powered Chinese name generator for career, travel, or living in China

faq.q1: Is this just a direct translation of my name?
faq.a1: No, our AI model generates names that truly suit you based on your personal info, profession, personality, and preferences.
faq.q2: Can I use this name on official documents?
faq.a2: Yes, but we recommend double-checking with Chinese colleagues or friends before official use.
faq.q3: What is included in the name card?
faq.a3: You will receive Chinese characters, pinyin (pronunciation), detailed meaning of each character, and why it suits you. It will also include your zodiac, five elements, lucky numbers, etc.
faq.a4: Yes, this service is currently free.
faq.q5: Are one or two-character names better?
faq.a5: It depends on your surname and personal preference; two-character names are mainstream nowadays.

story.p1: The birth of tripcngo.com stems from our passion for China and obsession with the travel experience.
story.p2: We believe travel should be spontaneous and full of surprises, not just checking in at popular spots.
story.p3: With the 240-hour transit visa-free policy, more friends are visiting China. However, challenges like language, internet, and payments can be daunting.
story.p4: So we created tripcngo.com to bridge the information gap, combining our travel expertise with AI technology.
story.p5: From visa guides to smart helpers, we help you discover the real and beautiful China.

intro.p1: The People\'s Republic of China is one of the oldest countries in the world, with 5,000 years of civilization.
intro.p2: Located in East Asia, China was founded in 1949. It is one of the world\'s most populous countries with 1.4 billion people and an area of 9.6 million sq km (3rd largest).
intro.p3: The terrain follows a three-step ladder from the high Qinghai-Tibet Plateau in the west to the low plains and coastline in the east. This fall creates diverse landscapes including deserts, plateaus, and basins.
intro.p4: China is divided into 23 provinces, 5 autonomous regions, 4 municipalities (Beijing, Shanghai, Tianjin, Chongqing), and 2 SARs (Hong Kong, Macau).
intro.p5: Unified multi-ethnic country with 56 ethnic groups. Han is the majority. Mandarin is the common language.
intro.p6: Chinese culture is profound and colorful. It holds a significant position in world culture. Traditional arts include poetry, opera, calligraphy, and painting.
intro.title: Intro to China
